channel.title: Glossary — paype.cc
channel.description: Plain-English definitions of every paype.cc term: MSB, KYC/KYB, UBO, ACH, SWIFT, SEPA, cryptographic audit trail, and more.

item.title: ACH — Automated Clearing House
item.description: ACH is the US electronic bank-to-bank payment network. paype uses SWIFT and SEPA for bank transfers instead.

item.title: AML — Anti-Money Laundering
item.description: The laws and procedures that prevent criminals from disguising illegal funds. paype runs continuous AML screening.

item.title: Big Band — paype's Mid-Volume Tier
item.description: Big Band is paype's mid-volume discount — 25% off all fees for the rest of any calendar month where your fees exceed $500. Resets monthly, triggers automatically.

item.title: BSA — Bank Secrecy Act
item.description: The primary US anti-money laundering law. paype operates under BSA requirements via its FinCEN registration.

item.title: Cryptographic audit trail
item.description: A tamper-evident record of every transaction on your account. Every entry carries a cryptographic signature you can independently verify.

item.title: FinCEN — Financial Crimes Enforcement Network
item.description: A bureau of the US Treasury and the primary regulator of Money Services Businesses in the United States.

item.title: KYC / KYB — Know Your Customer / Know Your Business
item.description: KYC and KYB are the identity verification steps required to open a paype account. Automated in-app — most accounts verified in minutes.

item.title: Melody — paype's Default Tier
item.description: Melody is paype's starting tier — standard rates, no minimum fees, no application. Every paype account starts on Melody and upgrades automatically as volume grows.

item.title: Melusina
item.description: Melusina is the open-source financial infrastructure powering paype.cc — multi-party custody, cryptographic signing, compliance automation, and cross-border payment rails.

item.title: MSB — Money Services Business
item.description: An MSB is a FinCEN-regulated financial service provider. paype.cc is an MSB — not a bank. This matters for what your funds are and are not protected by.

item.title: OFAC — Office of Foreign Assets Control
item.description: OFAC is the US Treasury agency that administers economic sanctions. paype screens every transaction against OFAC lists in real time.

item.title: Orchestra — paype's Top Volume Tier
item.description: Orchestra is paype's highest volume discount tier — 40% off all fees after you hit $1,000 in cumulative yearly fees. Triggers automatically, no contract.

item.title: Payment Rails
item.description: Payment rails are the underlying networks that move money — SWIFT, SEPA, card networks, and blockchain. paype connects the major international rails through one account.

item.title: Payment Request
item.description: A payment request is a hosted link you create in paype and share with anyone. They pay by card, bank, or crypto — no paype account needed on their end.

item.title: Segregated Accounts
item.description: Your paype funds are held in accounts segregated from paype's operating capital. paype's business finances cannot touch your balance.

item.title: SEPA — Single Euro Payments Area
item.description: SEPA is the EU-wide payment network for euro transfers. On paype, SEPA outbound costs from $3 + 0.21% on Orchestra.

item.title: Sub-Accounts
item.description: Sub-accounts are separate wallets inside your paype account — each with its own balance, card, spending limits, and user permissions. Unlimited, free to create.

item.title: SWIFT — Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication
item.description: SWIFT is the global messaging network for international bank transfers. On paype, SWIFT outbound starts at $15 + 0.45% on Orchestra, reaching 170+ countries.

item.title: Tokenization
item.description: Tokenization replaces your real card number with a device-specific token for every transaction. Merchants never see your actual card details.

item.title: UBO — Ultimate Beneficial Owner
item.description: A UBO is any person who ultimately owns or controls 25% or more of a business. Each UBO must complete identity verification when a business opens a paype account.
